skill.name: bmi-bsa-calculator
skill.description: Calculate Body Mass Index (BMI) and Body Surface Area (BSA) for clinical assessment, obesity screening, and chemotherapy dosing. Supports multiple BSA formulas (DuBois, Mosteller, Haycock), WHO weight classification, pediatric calculations, and metric/imperial input.
skill.summary: Clinical BMI and BSA calculator for health assessment, obesity screening, and chemotherapy dosing with multiple validated formulas.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 18 weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives: the Python ** operator is mathematical exponentiation used in BSA formulas (DuBois: weight^0.425 x height^0.725), not cryptography. In SKILL.md, ** is markdown bold syntax. All 32 shell backtick execution findings are false positives from markdown code block formatting showing CLI usage examples. One legitimate finding confirmed: Python file write at main.py:165 writes calculation results to a user-specified output file path via the --output flag, which is standard CLI behavior. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or prompt injection detected. The dynamic audit result grades this skill Production Ready (86/100) with deployable: true.

low_findings.title: Python file write to user-specified path
low_findings.description: The CLI writes calculation results to a file path provided by the user via the --output flag. This is legitimate CLI behavior for saving results. The file path is user-controlled and no path traversal or injection is present.

content.user_title: Calculate BMI and BSA for Clinical Assessment and Chemotherapy Dosing
content.value_statement: Medical professionals need quick, accurate BMI and BSA values for clinical screening and oncology dosing. This skill calculates both metrics using multiple validated formulas with proper clinical context and safety guardrails.
actual_capabilities: Calculate BMI from weight and height with WHO weight classification
Estimate BSA using multiple validated formulas (DuBois, Mosteller, Haycock, Gehan-George)
Calculate chemotherapy and medication doses based on BSA
Support pediatric calculations with age-appropriate norms
Accept metric and imperial unit input with automatic conversion
Refuse out-of-scope requests for diagnosis or treatment advice
limitations: BMI does not distinguish between muscle mass and fat mass
All BSA formulas are approximations with 10 to 15 percent normal variation
Cannot provide clinical diagnoses or treatment recommendations
Standard formulas may be inaccurate for amputees or during pregnancy

use_cases.title: Obesity Screening in Primary Care
use_cases.description: A physician calculates BMI and WHO classification for a patient during a routine checkup to screen for weight-related health risks.

use_cases.title: Chemotherapy Dosing in Oncology
use_cases.description: An oncologist calculates BSA using the DuBois or Gehan-George formula to determine the correct chemotherapy drug dose for a patient.

use_cases.title: Pediatric Growth Assessment
use_cases.description: A pediatrician calculates BMI-for-age percentiles for a child using the Haycock BSA formula with age-appropriate norms.

prompt_templates.title: Basic BMI Calculation
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate BMI for a person weighing 70 kg with a height of 175 cm. Include the WHO weight classification.

prompt_templates.title: BMI and BSA with Formula Selection
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs both BMI and BSA with a specific formula
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate BMI and BSA using the Mosteller formula for a male patient weighing 85 kg with height 180 cm. Classify the BMI according to WHO standards.

prompt_templates.title: Chemotherapy Dosing Calculation
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate BSA using the DuBois formula for a patient weighing 65 kg with height 165 cm. Then calculate the carboplatin dose at 400 mg per m2 with a maximum dose of 800 mg.

prompt_templates.title: Pediatric BMI with Age Adjustment
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate BMI and BSA for a 6-year-old female child weighing 22 kg with height 115 cm. Use the Haycock formula which is appropriate for children. Apply pediatric norms.

output_examples.output: BMI: 22.9 kg/m2 - Normal weight. BSA (DuBois): 1.84 m2. This BMI falls within the healthy range (18.5 to 24.9). Note that BMI is a screening tool and does not distinguish between muscle and fat mass.

output_examples.input: Calculate chemotherapy dose for patient 65 kg, 165 cm, carboplatin 400 mg/m2
output_examples.output: BSA (DuBois): 1.72 m2. Calculated carboplatin dose: 688 mg. Maximum dose cap of 800 mg not exceeded. Double-check all chemotherapy calculations independently.

output_examples.input: Calculate BMI and BSA for a 6-year-old child, 22 kg, 115 cm
output_examples.output: BMI: 16.6 kg/m2 - Normal weight. BSA (Haycock): 0.82 m2. This is within expected range for a 6-year-old female child. Haycock formula used as recommended for pediatric patients.

best_practices: Always verify that weight is in kilograms and height is in centimeters before calculating to avoid unit conversion errors.
Use the Haycock formula for pediatric patients under 12 years old and the DuBois formula for standard adult calculations.
Double-check all chemotherapy dosing calculations independently and correlate BSA-based doses with clinical guidelines.
anti_patterns: Using BMI as a standalone diagnostic tool instead of a screening metric to assess individual health.
Applying adult BSA formulas to pediatric patients without switching to the age-appropriate Haycock formula.
Requesting clinical diagnoses or treatment recommendations from this skill which is designed for calculations only.

faq.question: What formulas does this skill support for BSA calculation?
faq.answer: This skill supports DuBois, Mosteller, Haycock, Gehan-George, and Yu formulas for body surface area calculation.

faq.question: Can this skill diagnose medical conditions?
faq.answer: No. This skill calculates BMI and BSA values only. It cannot diagnose conditions or provide treatment advice.

faq.question: What is the difference between BMI and BSA?
faq.answer: BMI measures body mass relative to height for weight screening. BSA estimates the surface area of the body and is used for medication dosing especially in oncology.

faq.question: Does this skill support imperial units?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill accepts weight in pounds and height in inches and converts them to metric values for calculation.

faq.question: Which BSA formula should I use for children?
faq.answer: Use the Haycock formula for children under 12 years. It is validated for pediatric body surface area estimation.

faq.question: Can this skill help with chemotherapy dosing?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill calculates BSA and can compute drug doses based on BSA and the provided dose per square meter. Always verify calculations independently.
